# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a TypeScript Express endpoint for serving user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via req.params.filename. Use fs.readFile with path.join to read from uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization. Type the filename parameter as string. This is for our file storage API.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for path traversal vulnerabilities and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any direct use of user-controlled filenames or paths, missing canonicalization, or missing base-directory boundary checks.
4. Resolve/canonicalize paths, reject invalid paths, and enforce that file access remains inside the intended directory.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: path_traversal

import express, { Request, Response } from "express";
import fs from "fs";
import path from "path";

const app = express();

const UPLOADS_DIR = path.resolve(__dirname, "uploads");

app.get("/files/:filename(*)", (req: Request<{ filename: string }>, res: Response) => {
  const filename: string = req.params.filename;

  // Reject obviously malicious patterns early
  if (!filename || filename.length === 0) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "Filename is required" });
    return;
  }

  // Reject null bytes which can be used to truncate paths
  if (filename.includes("\0")) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "Invalid filename" });
    return;
  }

  // Resolve the requested path to an absolute canonical path
  const requestedPath = path.resolve(UPLOADS_DIR, filename);

  // Canonicalize using realpath is ideal but the file must exist first.
  // Instead, we normalize and check that the resolved path starts with the uploads directory.
  const normalizedUploadsDir = path.normalize(UPLOADS_DIR) + path.sep;
  const normalizedRequestedPath = path.normalize(requestedPath);

  // Ensure the resolved path is strictly within the uploads directory
  if (
    normalizedRequestedPath !== path.normalize(UPLOADS_DIR) &&
    !normalizedRequestedPath.startsWith(normalizedUploadsDir)
  ) {
    res.status(403).json({ error: "Access denied: path traversal detected" });
    return;
  }

  // Use fs.realpath to resolve symlinks and verify the canonical path is still within bounds
  fs.realpath(normalizedRequestedPath, (realpathErr, canonicalPath) => {
    if (realpathErr) {
      if (realpathErr.code === "ENOENT") {
        res.status(404).json({ error: "File not found" });
      } else {
        res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error" });
      }
      return;
    }

    // Re-check after symlink resolution
    fs.realpath(UPLOADS_DIR, (baseDirErr, canonicalUploadsDir) => {
      if (baseDirErr) {
        res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error" });
        return;
      }

      const canonicalBaseDirWithSep = canonicalUploadsDir + path.sep;

      if (
        canonicalPath !== canonicalUploadsDir &&
        !canonicalPath.startsWith(canonicalBaseDirWithSep)
      ) {
        res.status(403).json({ error: "Access denied: path traversal detected" });
        return;
      }

      // Ensure we're serving a file, not a directory
      fs.stat(canonicalPath, (statErr, stats) => {
        if (statErr) {
          res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error" });
          return;
        }

        if (!stats.isFile()) {
          res.status(400).json({ error: "Requested path is not a file" });
          return;
        }

        fs.readFile(canonicalPath, (readErr, data) => {
          if (readErr) {
            if (readErr.code === "ENOENT") {
              res.status(404).json({ error: "File not found" });
            } else if (readErr.code === "EACCES") {
              res.status(403).json({ error: "Access denied" });
            } else {
              res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error" });
            }
            return;
          }

          // Determine content type based on extension
          const ext = path.extname(canonicalPath).toLowerCase();
          const mimeTypes: Record<string, string> = {
            ".html": "text/html",
            ".css": "text/css",
            ".js": "application/javascript",
            ".json": "application/json",
            ".png": "image/png",
            ".jpg": "image/jpeg",
            ".jpeg": "image/jpeg",
            ".gif": "image/gif",
            ".svg": "image/svg+xml",
            ".pdf": "application/pdf",
            ".txt": "text/plain",
            ".zip": "application/zip",
            ".mp4": "video/mp4",
            ".mp3": "audio/mpeg",
          };

          const contentType = mimeTypes[ext] || "application/octet-stream";

          res.setHeader("Content-Type", contentType);
          res.setHeader("Content-Length", data.length);
          // Prevent browsers from MIME-sniffing
          res.setHeader("X-Content-Type-Options", "nosniff");
          res.status(200).send(data);
        });
      });
    });
  });
});

const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;
app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`File storage API server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;
